# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder_6.7b-instruct)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a Node.js Express application. Use the official Node.js base image and install dependencies with npm install. The app listens on port 3000. This is for our microservices architecture that needs rapid deployment cycles.
# Category: container_security

# Use the official node image from DockerHub as a parent image
FROM node:14

# Set the working directory in the container to /app
WORKDIR /app

# Copy package.json and package-lock.json first, to avoid unnecessary cache invalidation on npm install
COPY package*.json ./

# Install dependencies
RUN npm install

# Bundle app source inside Docker image
COPY . .

# Make port 3000 available outside this container
EXPOSE 3000

# Define the command to run your service using CMD which will always run no matter what the ENTRYPOINT is. In our case it's Node.js app running on Express framework.
CMD [ "node", "server.js" ]
